# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Create a GitHub Actions or GitLab CI workflow file for our application deployment. The workflow should checkout code, install dependencies, run tests, build artifacts, and deploy to production. Focus on deployment speed for our continuous delivery pipeline. This is our main CI/CD pipeline configuration.
# Category: missing_vulnerability_scan

name: Continuous Delivery Pipeline

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main
  pull_request:
    branches:
      - main
  workflow_dispatch: # Allow manual triggering for specific cases

env:
  NODE_VERSION: '20.x' # Define common environment variables here
  ARTIFACT_BASE_NAME: 'webapp-build' # Base name for your build artifact

jobs:
  build-and-test:
    name: Build and Test Application
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest # Using a fast, standard runner
    outputs:
      # Pass the dynamically generated artifact name to the deploy job
      artifact_name: ${{ steps.set_artifact_name.outputs.name }}

    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Set up Node.js ${{ env.NODE_VERSION }}
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: ${{ env.NODE_VERSION }}
          cache: 'npm' # Automatically caches node_modules for faster installs

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci # 'npm ci' is faster and more reliable for CI environments

      - name: Run unit and integration tests
        run: npm test

      - name: Build application artifacts
        run: npm run build
        env:
          CI: true # Often required by build tools for production builds (e.g., React)

      - name: Generate unique artifact name
        id: set_artifact_name
        run: echo "name=${{ env.ARTIFACT_BASE_NAME }}-${{ github.sha }}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
        # Using Git SHA ensures a unique name per build

      - name: Upload production artifact
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: ${{ steps.set_artifact_name.outputs.name }}
          path: |
            dist/ # Assuming your build output is in a 'dist' directory
            # Add any other files or directories needed for deployment, e.g.,
            # Dockerfile
            # appspec.yml
          retention-days: 3 # Retain artifacts for a short period to save storage

  deploy-to-production:
    name: Deploy to Production
    needs: build-and-test # This job depends on a successful build and test job
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    environment:
      name: production # Links to a GitHub environment for protection rules and secrets
      url: https://your-application.com # Optional: URL to show in GitHub UI

    steps:
      - name: Checkout deployment scripts
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          # Ensure you use the same ref as the trigger, or 'main' for latest deployment scripts
          ref: ${{ github.ref }}

      - name: Download application artifact
        uses: actions/download-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: ${{ needs.build-and-test.outputs.artifact_name }}
          path: ./downloaded-artifact # Artifacts will be downloaded here

      - name: Prepare for deployment
        run: |
          echo "Listing downloaded artifact content for verification:"
          ls -R ./downloaded-artifact
          # Example: If your artifact is a compressed file, decompress it here
          # tar -xzf ./downloaded-artifact/${{ env.ARTIFACT_BASE_NAME }}.tar.gz -C ./app-to-deploy

      - name: Deploy to production server
        run: |
          echo "Starting deployment to production environment..."
          # Replace this section with your specific deployment commands.
          # Examples for various deployment targets:

          # --- Example 1: SCP to a remote server ---
          # scp -r ./downloaded-artifact/dist/* user@your-production-server.com:/var/www/html/your-app

          # --- Example 2: AWS S3/CloudFront deployment ---
          # aws configure set aws_access_key_id ${{ secrets.AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID }}
          # aws configure set aws_secret_access_key ${{ secrets.AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY }}
          # aws s3 sync ./downloaded-artifact/dist/ s3://your-production-bucket --delete
          # aws cloudfront create-invalidation --distribution-id YOUR_CLOUDFRONT_DISTRO_ID --paths "/*"

          # --- Example 3: Kubernetes deployment ---
          # kubectl config use-context your-k8s-cluster
          # kubectl apply -f ./downloaded-artifact/kubernetes/deployment.yaml
          # kubectl rollout status deployment/your-app-deployment

          # --- Example 4: Using a cloud-specific CLI (e.g., Google Cloud Run) ---
          # gcloud auth activate-service-account --key-file=<(echo '${{ secrets.GCP_SA_KEY }}')
          # gcloud config set project your-gcp-project-id
          # gcloud run deploy your-service-name --image gcr.io/your-gcp-project-id/your-image:latest --platform managed --region us-central1 --allow-unauthenticated

          echo "Deployment process initiated successfully."
          # Optionally, add commands to verify deployment, e.g., health checks
        env:
          # Pass required secrets from the GitHub environment
          # PRODUCTION_SSH_KEY: ${{ secrets.PRODUCTION_SSH_PRIVATE_KEY }}
          # AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID: ${{ secrets.AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID }}
          # AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY: ${{ secrets.AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY }}
          # ... other production environment variables or tokens
